campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell City Council accepted a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help pay for youth recreation scholarships. The council also voted on extending an emergency rule (Ordinance 2326) and moved forward with plans to build a bike and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including applying for grant funding to help pay for it.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen for youth scholarships
Extension of emergency Ordinance 2326
Hamilton Avenue bike/pedestrian bridge over Highway 17
Grant application for bridge planning and construction funding

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council introduced the proposed ordinance and skipped the standard first reading process to move things along faster. The council directed staff to prepare the ordinance for a final vote at the next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked (first reading waived)
Ordinance scheduled for adoption vote at next regular meeting
Specific ordinance topic not detailed in agenda summary

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council met to finalize their weed control efforts from 2026 and establish new rules for how the council operates. They also approved flying Progress Pride and Juneteenth flags at city buildings, discussed affordable housing policies, and heard updates from the local transit authority about transportation.
keyTopics: Completion of city-wide weed removal program
New official procedures for how City Council meetings will run
Affordable housing protections for existing residents
Progress Pride and Juneteenth flag displays at city buildings
Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority updates

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council approved several contracts and agreements to move forward with various projects and services. The council authorized the Town Manager to sign construction work with QLM, Inc., hire professional services from Dudek, and join a statewide insurance pool (CJPIA) to manage the city's insurance needs. Additionally, the council made updates to existing agreements with other service providers.
keyTopics: Construction contract approval with QLM, Inc.
Professional services agreement with Dudek
Joining California Joint Powers Insurance Authority for better insurance coverage
Updates to existing agreements with multiple vendors
Art transfer agreement (details incomplete in agenda)

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: San José city leaders reviewed progress on programs that help young people in the community, including youth empowerment initiatives and summer safety programs. They also received updates on how well the city's code enforcement is working and discussed plans to improve library buildings and services.
keyTopics: Youth Empowerment Alliance and Safe Summer Initiative Grant programs
Children and Youth Services Master Plan progress report
Code Enforcement Annual Report performance review
Library facility improvements and customer experience upgrades

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: This Mountain View City Council meeting on April 7, 2026 was held with both in-person and online options for residents. The meeting allowed community members to participate virtually via Zoom or phone to ask questions and speak during public comment periods.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom and phone dial-in
Public comment procedures for remote participants
How to raise your hand to speak during the meeting

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held their regular meeting on April 9, 2026, starting with the Pledge of Allegiance. Three commissioners shared personal stories about their experiences in Sunnyvale, and the group approved the official notes from their previous meeting in March.
keyTopics: Human Relations Commission regular meeting
Personal stories shared by three commissioners
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes
Community engagement and civic participation

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss several community programs including a food initiative called Budget and Bites, a youth-led organization called Unmuted Tabs, and summer job opportunities for young people in the city. The council also approved minutes from their previous March meeting and opened the floor for residents to speak about any city matters for up to three minutes each.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites food program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth-led organization
Summer jobs availability for young residents
Public comment period for residents

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara City Council is holding meetings both in-person and online to make it easier for residents to participate. If you want to attend or speak, you can join via Zoom or phone, or submit written comments through the city's website before the meeting.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format (in-person and remote options available)
Zoom participation available via video or phone call
Written public comments can be submitted online before the meeting
Meeting details and agenda available on City Council website

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to handle several administrative and financial matters affecting the city. Key decisions included approving tax board participation, considering fee waivers for a community educational organization, moving forward with a construction project, and reviewing how federal housing and community development funds will be spent.
keyTopics: Franchise Tax Board participation authorization
Fee waiver and donation requests for Milpitas Community Educational
Construction project approval and contract award
Federal housing and community development funding allocation
New council workplan items and priorities

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to review and approve the city's gas utility budget and rates for the next fiscal year. The main decision involved approving new gas rate schedules for residential customers, master-metered buildings, and large commercial users, along with transferring money between the gas utility fund and the city's general fund.
keyTopics: Approval of FY 2027 gas utility budget and financial forecast
Changes to residential gas rates (Schedule G-1)
Changes to commercial and master-metered gas rates (Schedules G-2 and G-3)
Transfer of funds from gas utility reserves to general city budget
Late agenda items added to the meeting
